_note: SAMPLE DATA — manually maintained student review summaries for planning reference only. This is NOT official UOA data. Reviews are compiled from publicly shared student experiences and are representative samples only.

negativeComments: Content can feel dry at times
ACCTG 102.tipsForFutureStudents: Practice questions every week — don't leave them until exam time. The textbook is actually useful for this one.

negativeComments: Challenging concepts in second half of semester
Group presentation can be stressful with a bad group
ACCTG 211.tipsForFutureStudents: Form a study group early. The financial accounting standards take time to sink in — review weekly.

positiveComments: Very practical and applicable to real work
ACCTG 221.tipsForFutureStudents: Don't skip the workshops — they're where the concepts click. The Excel skills you learn are valuable for internships.

positiveComments: Practical tools you can use immediately
BUSAN 200.tipsForFutureStudents: If you haven't done STATS 108, brush up on basic statistics before the semester starts.

BUSAN 300.tipsForFutureStudents: Start the group project as early as possible. The data wrangling toolkit is the most valuable part — don't skip labs.

BUSINESS 111.tipsForFutureStudents: This is a great first-semester course. Participate actively in workshops and choose your group members carefully.

negativeComments: Team project can dominate your schedule
BUSINESS 112.tipsForFutureStudents: Block out regular Maker Space time. The presentation skills you develop here are valuable for every other BCom course.

positiveComments: Good mix of theory and cases
negativeComments: Some legal concepts can be dense
COMLAW 101.tipsForFutureStudents: Read the cases before lectures. The workshops are where you learn to apply the law — attendance is essential.

negativeComments: Final exam is worth 50% — high pressure
Some topics require multiple readings to understand
COMLAW 201.tipsForFutureStudents: Don't leave the problem question assignment to the last week. Practice past exam questions — the format is consistent.

positiveComments: Light workload compared to other BCom courses
negativeComments: Can feel like common sense at times
Not as quantitative as some students want
ECON 151.tipsForFutureStudents: Keep up with the news — it makes the course much more interesting. A good choice for your first semester.

ECON 152.tipsForFutureStudents: Do the tutorial problem sets every week. The lab worksheets are excellent exam preparation.

ECON 201.tipsForFutureStudents: Make sure you're comfortable with calculus and algebra. The textbook is essential reading — don't rely on lectures alone.

positiveComments: Very practical for personal and professional life
negativeComments: Some formulas take practice to master
FINANCE 251.tipsForFutureStudents: Get the financial calculator early and practice with it. Do ALL the practice problems — the exam follows a similar format.

FINANCE 261.tipsForFutureStudents: Take FINANCE 251 first or concurrently. The stock simulation is fun but don't let it distract from the theory.

negativeComments: Can feel basic if you're tech-savvy
Group assignments depend heavily on team
INFOSYS 110.tipsForFutureStudents: Don't underestimate the Excel sections — they're tested in the final. The database module is a good preview of INFOSYS 222.

negativeComments: SQL can be challenging at first
INFOSYS 222.tipsForFutureStudents: Practice SQL every week — it's like learning a language. The ERD design skills are just as important as the coding.

INTBUS 201.tipsForFutureStudents: Read the case studies before tutorials. The international perspective is valuable even if you don't major in INTBUS.

positiveComments: Develops critical thinking about global markets
INTBUS 305.tipsForFutureStudents: Stay on top of the readings — they're essential for the essays. Follow international business news throughout the semester.

positiveComments: Really interesting content about work and people
negativeComments: No final exam — relies heavily on written assessments
MGMT 223.tipsForFutureStudents: This is a great course if you enjoy writing and reflection. The team report is a major component — choose your topic carefully.

positiveComments: Group project is hands-on and realistic
Great for anyone interested in consumer insights
negativeComments: Group research report is a big commitment
MKTG 202.tipsForFutureStudents: Start thinking about your research topic early. The survey design phase takes longer than you think.

negativeComments: AI-integrated journal is a new assessment format
MKTG 301.tipsForFutureStudents: Take MKTG 202 first. The journal assessment rewards consistent work — don't leave it to the end.

negativeComments: Some mathematical topics can be dry
OPSMGT 255.tipsForFutureStudents: The Excel skills are very practical — practice them. This course pairs well with BUSAN 200.
